macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of the Daf: Reuven and the Sons of Eli

micro_segments.title: Opening: Summary of figures discussed (Reuven, Eli's sons, David, Shlomo, Yoash)
Reuven case: Yesterday's discussion affirmed, but debate exists on whether he sinned
Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani quoting Rabbi Yonatan: anyone saying Eli's sons sinned is mistaken
Lecturer's ethical concerns: papering over biblical sins, abuse of authority, and religious leaders

macro_segments.title: Eli's Sons (Chofni and Phinehas): Delaying Sacrifices Rather Than Adultery

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: Phinehas did not sin; Chofni did, but it was reinterpreted as delaying sacrifices
How the sin is reframed: delaying women's sacrifices prevented them from being with husbands
Textual difficulties: parsing yishkevun (plural vs. singular) and other manuscript variants
Phinehas held accountable for not protesting his brother Chofni's behavior

macro_segments.title: Samuel's Sons: Not Taking Bribes but Neglecting the People

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani: anyone saying Samuel's sons sinned is mistaken
The sons didn't go in their father's ways: comparison to Samuel's circuit of judging
Reframing 'going after gain' as sitting in cities for patronage rather than outright bribery
Tannaitic debate: various interpretations of how they profited (ma'aser, gifts, extra box)

macro_segments.title: David: Adultery with Bathsheba Reinterpreted and Receiving Lashon Hara

micro_segments.title: Anyone saying David sinned regarding Bathsheba is mistaken; exploring the get (divorce document) given to soldiers
Soldiers departing for war gave conditional or unconditional gets; Bathsheba was technically free
Uriah as mored b'malchus (rebel against the king); David's sin was not using proper court
Rav's position: only sin was Uriah; other voices note census and accepting lashon hara
David accepting lashon hara from Tziva about Mephibosheth and the consequences for the kingdom
Debate: Rav says David accepted slander; Shmuel says he saw supporting evidence against Mephibosheth
Result: Heavenly voice announces kingdom will split (Rehoboam and Jeroboam) due to accepting lashon hara

macro_segments.title: Solomon: Building Altars Reinterpreted as Allowance Rather Than Personal Action

micro_segments.title: Anyone saying Solomon sinned is mistaken; his heart was not as whole as David's
Reframing building altars: wives led him astray but he didn't actually worship idols
Textual argument: future tense 'yivneh' (will build) means intention, not action; comparison to Joshua
Alternative explanation via Reb Natan: altars built by others; held accountable only for not protesting
Rav Yehuda quoting Shmuel: better to serve idolatry actively than passively allow it
Historical consequence: Solomon's marriage to Pharaoh's daughter led to founding of Rome

macro_segments.title: Jeroboam and Josiah: Building Idolatry and Great Repentance

micro_segments.title: Jeroboam instituted golden calves in Bethel and Dan; this led to development of Rome
Josiah did not sin; his 'return' to God meant reversing unjust court decisions after finding the Torah scroll
Rav disagrees: Josiah was a great ba'al teshuvah (master of repentance); none greater in his generation
Rav Yosef: examples of great ba'alei teshuvah in later generations (Abba, Acha, Ukva bar Nechemiah)
